meta.title: Ethics & Moral Understanding Assessment
meta.description: A self-reflection test that explores how you tend to respond in everyday moral situations across 10 dimensions of ethics.

levels.label: Exemplary
levels.desc: You consistently choose the principled path, even when it costs you something. Your moral compass is a strong, reliable guide.

levels.label: Principled
levels.desc: You have a strong, well-formed sense of right and wrong and act on it in most situations. A few edge cases are still worth reflecting on.

levels.label: Conscientious
levels.desc: You generally lean toward the ethical choice, but context and convenience sometimes sway your decisions.

levels.label: Developing
levels.desc: Your ethical responses are situational — strong in some areas, inconsistent in others. Worth some intentional reflection.

levels.label: Inconsistent
levels.desc: Convenience or self-interest often outweighs principle in your responses. Small, deliberate changes could make a big difference.

levels.label: Needs Reflection
levels.desc: Your answers suggest ethical considerations rarely drive your choices right now. This is a good starting point for honest self-reflection.

questions.text: You accidentally damage a colleague's belongings and no one saw it happen. How likely are you to admit it and offer to fix or replace it?
A friend asks if you like their new haircut, and you honestly don't. How likely are you to tell them the truth tactfully rather than just saying yes?
You could get away with claiming a few extra hours on your timesheet that you didn't actually work. How likely are you to do this?
You could skip a long line by pretending you have an appointment you don't actually have. How likely are you to do this?
Your manager asks for your honest opinion on a decision you disagree with, even though simply agreeing would be easier. How likely are you to give your honest opinion anyway?
You notice a cashier gave you extra change by mistake. How likely are you to point out the error and return the money?
A friend is being deceived by someone else, and you know the truth. How likely are you to tell your friend, even if it causes conflict?
You're filling out an application and could exaggerate a qualification to seem more impressive. How likely are you to do this?
You made a mistake at work that no one has noticed yet. How likely are you to proactively report it?
Someone asks for feedback on their business idea, and it has serious flaws. How likely are you to give an honest, constructive assessment instead of just being polite?
Your friend asks if their cooking tastes good, and it's mediocre at best. How likely are you to give a kind but honest answer instead of empty praise?
You billed a client for hours you're not fully certain you worked. How likely are you to double-check and correct the invoice before sending it?
A teacher gives you partial credit for an answer you know was purely a lucky guess. How likely are you to mention that you guessed?
You return a borrowed tool in worse condition than you received it. How likely are you to tell the owner rather than stay quiet?
You could inflate your résumé with a certification you never actually completed. How likely are you to do this?
A cashier undercharges you for a large purchase and doesn't notice. How likely are you to point it out?
You're asked in a job interview about a gap in your work history you'd rather not fully explain. How likely are you to answer truthfully instead of inventing a cover story?
Your partner asks whether you actually liked a gift they gave you, and you didn't. How likely are you to share your honest feelings gently?
You accidentally sent a confidential email to the wrong person. How likely are you to tell your manager immediately rather than hope no one notices?
A friend lends you money and later forgets about it. How likely are you to remind them and pay it back anyway?
You're asked to sign off on a report that contains a number you know is slightly wrong. How likely are you to flag the error before signing?
You could tell a small lie to get out of a social obligation you don't want to attend. How likely are you to do this?
A friend asks your honest opinion about their relationship, and you have real concerns. How likely are you to share them respectfully?
You notice a warranty claim you're filing stretches the truth slightly in your favor. How likely are you to correct it before submitting?
A coworker's project review is due, and being fully honest may hurt their feelings. How likely are you to still give accurate feedback?
A store clerk hands you a receipt showing a discount you didn't actually qualify for. How likely are you to mention the mistake?
You're asked how a shared project went, and your honest answer would reflect poorly on a teammate. How likely are you to describe it accurately anyway?
A friend asks you to vouch for something about them that isn't quite true. How likely are you to decline?
You could quietly take credit in a meeting for an idea that was actually a friend's suggestion. How likely are you to do this?
Your doctor asks how consistently you've been following a treatment plan, and the honest answer is 'not very.' How likely are you to admit it?
You promised a friend you'd be at their event, but a better offer comes up. How likely are you to tell them the real reason if you can't make it?
A customer asks if a product you're selling has any downsides. How likely are you to mention the genuine drawbacks?
You're filling out an anonymous survey with no way anyone could ever know your real answers. How likely are you to still answer truthfully?
You notice a factual error in something you posted online that people have been sharing. How likely are you to correct it publicly?
A friend compliments an outfit you actually think looks bad before an important event. How likely are you to gently tell them the truth?
You could let people keep believing something false about you because it makes you look impressive. How likely are you to let the misunderstanding stand instead of correcting it?
You're negotiating a sale, and revealing a fact about the item would lower its value. How likely are you to disclose it anyway?
A friend asks whether you talked about them behind their back at a party. How likely are you to admit it honestly if you did?
You're asked to estimate how long a task will take, and your honest estimate is longer than what people want to hear. How likely are you to give the accurate estimate anyway?
You witness a car accident and are asked what you saw. How likely are you to describe events exactly as they happened, even if it implicates someone you know?
A subscription service accidentally stops charging you. How likely are you to report the billing error rather than enjoy it for free?
Your child asks if you ever broke rules at their age. How likely are you to answer honestly rather than pretend you were perfect?
You could dodge a direct question by giving a technically true but misleading response. How likely are you to do this?
You're asked for a reference for a former employee who underperformed. How likely are you to give a balanced, honest assessment rather than an inflated one?
A friend's business plan has a flaw you're the only one who's noticed. How likely are you to bring it up even if it's awkward?
You realize you misquoted someone in something you wrote publicly. How likely are you to issue a correction?
You're asked to confirm a rumor about yourself that happens to be true but embarrassing. How likely are you to confirm it rather than deny it?
A friend asks if you've been avoiding them, and you have been. How likely are you to admit it honestly?
You're filling out a loan or rental application and could slightly round your income in your favor. How likely are you to report the exact, accurate figure instead?
You could avoid a difficult conversation by letting a partner assume something about you that isn't true. How likely are you to do this?
You see an elderly person struggling to carry a heavy bag down the street. How likely are you to stop and help them carry it home?
A coworker seems visibly upset but hasn't said anything. How likely are you to check in and ask if they're okay?
You're in a hurry, and a stranger asks you for directions. How likely are you to stop and help even though it will make you late?
A friend is going through a difficult breakup and calls you late at night to talk. How likely are you to stay on the phone and listen, even if you're tired?
You witness someone being mocked in a group setting. How likely are you to step in and support the person being targeted?
You notice a new coworker eating lunch alone every day. How likely are you to invite them to join you?
A homeless person asks you for help. How likely are you to offer them food, money, or some form of assistance?
Your sibling is anxious about a big decision. How likely are you to set aside your own opinions and truly listen to their concerns first?
You see a stranger crying in public. How likely are you to check if they're alright rather than walking past?
A classmate or colleague is struggling to understand something you find easy. How likely are you to patiently help them, even if it takes extra time?
You notice a coworker quietly struggling with a heavy workload. How likely are you to offer to help even if it's not your job?
A friend's pet passes away and they're grieving. How likely are you to check in on them over the following days, not just once?
You notice a child who seems lost in a crowded place. How likely are you to stop and help them find their family?
A stranger's car breaks down on the roadside. How likely are you to stop and offer help or call for assistance?
Your roommate is stressed about exams. How likely are you to offer to help them study or handle chores so they can focus?
You notice someone at a party standing alone and looking uncomfortable. How likely are you to go talk to them?
A coworker just received some bad personal news. How likely are you to give them space but check in gently later?
You see a delivery worker struggling with a heavy package up several flights of stairs. How likely are you to offer to help carry it?
A friend cancels plans last minute because they're overwhelmed. How likely are you to respond with understanding rather than frustration?
You're in a waiting room and notice someone crying alone. How likely are you to offer a comforting word or just sit near them?
A younger sibling or relative is nervous about their first day at a new school or job. How likely are you to take time to reassure them?
You see someone drop their groceries in a parking lot. How likely are you to stop and help pick them up?
A friend is venting about a problem that has an obvious solution to you. How likely are you to just listen first instead of jumping straight to advice?
You notice a classmate being left out of group activities. How likely are you to include them?
A coworker made an error that caused them visible embarrassment in a meeting. How likely are you to reassure them privately afterward?
You see an elderly neighbor struggling to shovel snow or do yard work. How likely are you to offer to help?
A friend going through a hard time apologizes for 'venting too much.' How likely are you to reassure them that you're glad they told you?
You notice someone on public transport who looks unwell. How likely are you to check if they're okay?
A colleague new to the country seems overwhelmed by unfamiliar customs. How likely are you to patiently help them adjust?
Your friend's business failed and they're embarrassed to talk about it. How likely are you to bring it up gently and offer support rather than avoid the topic?
You see a parent struggling to manage a crying child alone in public. How likely are you to offer a kind word or small assistance?
A friend shares a personal fear or insecurity with you. How likely are you to respond with patience rather than dismissiveness?
You notice a stray or injured animal on the street. How likely are you to try to help it or find someone who can?
A coworker is clearly anxious before a big presentation. How likely are you to offer encouragement beforehand?
You're the only one who notices a classmate didn't understand the homework instructions. How likely are you to quietly help them?
A friend going through a breakup wants to talk it through again, even though you've heard the story before. How likely are you to listen patiently once more?
You see someone struggling to communicate due to a language barrier. How likely are you to try to help bridge the gap?
A neighbor's power goes out during a storm, and you still have electricity. How likely are you to offer them a warm place to stay?
A friend is embarrassed about needing financial help. How likely are you to offer support in a way that preserves their dignity?
You notice a new student sitting alone at lunch. How likely are you to invite them to sit with you?
A coworker is overwhelmed and about to miss an important deadline. How likely are you to offer to help even though it's not required of you?
You see someone visibly nervous before a medical procedure in a waiting room. How likely are you to offer a reassuring word?
A friend feels guilty for asking you for help again. How likely are you to make them feel comfortable asking?
You notice a coworker who seems to be struggling with their mental health. How likely are you to gently check in rather than ignore it?
A stranger online shares that they're having a rough day. How likely are you to respond with a kind, supportive comment?
You see a person struggling to carry heavy bags onto a bus or train. How likely are you to offer a hand?
A friend's child is shy and struggling to make friends at a gathering. How likely are you to gently include them in activities?
You notice an acquaintance seems to be pulling away from friendships lately. How likely are you to reach out and check on them?
A coworker is nervous about asking for time off for a personal reason. How likely are you to encourage them to prioritize themselves?
You see someone being ignored while trying to speak up in a group discussion. How likely are you to make space for them to be heard?
You're dividing a reward among a team, and one member clearly did more work. How likely are you to make sure they get proportionally more credit or reward?
You witness a friend being treated unfairly because of their background. How likely are you to speak up in their defense?
You're in a position to hire someone, and a close friend applies but is less qualified than another candidate. How likely are you to choose the more qualified candidate anyway?
You see someone cut in line ahead of others who have been waiting much longer. How likely are you to say something?
A rule at work or school seems to disadvantage a particular group unfairly. How likely are you to raise this concern with someone who can change it?
You're judging a competition, and a friend's entry is weaker than a stranger's. How likely are you to score them fairly regardless of the relationship?
You have more resources or opportunities than others in your group. How likely are you to make sure everyone gets a fair chance, not just those already advantaged?
Two people give conflicting accounts of a conflict, and one is your close friend. How likely are you to withhold judgment until you know the full facts?
You benefit from a system or rule that you know is unfair to others. How likely are you to say something rather than stay quiet because it helps you?
A group project's grade depends on equal contribution, but one member barely participated. How likely are you to address this honestly rather than let it slide?
You're splitting a bill among friends, and one person ordered much more than everyone else. How likely are you to make sure the split reflects what each person actually owes?
You're on a hiring panel and notice a colleague's biased comment about a candidate's age. How likely are you to speak up against it?
A promotion decision favors someone with better connections over someone with better performance. How likely are you to advocate for the more deserving candidate?
You're assigning chores among housemates, and one person always ends up with the easiest tasks. How likely are you to push for a fairer rotation?
A teacher grades similar essays differently depending on who wrote them. How likely are you to point out the inconsistency if you notice it?
You're distributing limited event tickets among a group. How likely are you to use a fair method like a lottery rather than favoritism?
A friend group is being formed for a competition, and someone equally skilled is being left out. How likely are you to advocate for their inclusion?
You could bend the rules for someone you personally like while enforcing them strictly for everyone else. How likely are you to do this?
You witness an interviewer asking a candidate an inappropriate personal question unrelated to the job. How likely are you to raise a concern about it?
A community resource, like a scholarship, has criteria that unintentionally exclude a deserving group. How likely are you to flag it to those in charge?
You're refereeing or judging, and a mistake would benefit your own team. How likely are you to make the fair call anyway?
A landlord treats one tenant more strictly than others for the same violation. How likely are you to speak up if you witness it?
You're splitting inherited belongings with siblings and have first pick because you arrived first. How likely are you to let others have a fair say instead of taking advantage?
A group chat is making decisions without including someone who's normally part of the group. How likely are you to make sure they're looped in?
You notice that men and women on your team are being paid differently for the same role. How likely are you to raise the issue?
A queue-jumping customer gets served first because they complained loudly. How likely are you to point out that others were there first?
You could use insider knowledge to gain an advantage in a competition that's supposed to be a level playing field. How likely are you to do this?
You're on a committee awarding a grant, and a proposal from someone you dislike is objectively the strongest. How likely are you to vote for it anyway?
A sports team keeps benching a player unfairly despite their performance. How likely are you to advocate for them if you're a coach or teammate?
You're dividing tips among coworkers, and one person worked significantly more hours. How likely are you to adjust the split accordingly?
A neighborhood association enforces rules selectively based on who complains. How likely are you to push for consistent enforcement?
You overhear a manager mocking an employee's accent during a performance review. How likely are you to report or address this?
A raffle or contest at your workplace seems rigged toward certain people. How likely are you to question it if you notice?
You're grading peer submissions and one classmate's work resembles a friend's a bit too closely. How likely are you to evaluate it fairly rather than give them a pass?
A group decision excludes input from quieter members. How likely are you to make sure everyone gets a chance to weigh in?
You could quietly steer a decision in your favor using your position of authority. How likely are you to do this?
You're aware that a friend got a job through a personal connection rather than merit. How likely are you to acknowledge the concern if it comes up rather than defend it blindly?
A restaurant seats a large tipping group ahead of families that have been waiting longer. How likely are you to say something if you're the manager?
You're distributing overtime opportunities among your team. How likely are you to rotate them fairly rather than always picking favorites?
A club membership fee structure disadvantages lower-income applicants. How likely are you to advocate for a sliding scale if you have influence?
You're moderating an online forum and a rule violation involves a friend of yours. How likely are you to apply the same standard you would to a stranger?
A shared family decision benefits the sibling who complains the loudest. How likely are you to push for an equitable process instead?
You notice that feedback in performance reviews is harsher for certain employees without clear reason. How likely are you to raise this pattern?
A group of friends is deciding who gets the last spot on a trip. How likely are you to support a fair method like drawing names rather than picking a favorite?
You could let a friend cut in line at your workplace or event ahead of people who arrived earlier. How likely are you to do this?
A teacher's pet gets away with behavior that would get others in trouble. How likely are you to point out the double standard if you're in a position to?
You're setting prices for a service and could charge new customers more than regular ones unfairly. How likely are you to keep pricing consistent and transparent?
A community meeting only hears from the most vocal residents. How likely are you to make an effort to include quieter voices?
You're aware that a promotion criterion was changed at the last minute to favor a specific candidate. How likely are you to question the fairness of that change?
A group project divides credit unevenly despite equal effort. How likely are you to advocate for a fairer distribution of recognition?
You made a promise, but circumstances changed and keeping it is now inconvenient. How likely are you to follow through anyway?
A decision you made turned out badly, and blaming someone else would be easy. How likely are you to take responsibility instead?
No one is watching, and you have the chance to cut corners on a task. How likely are you to still do the job properly?
You committed to a volunteer shift, but a more fun opportunity comes up at the same time. How likely are you to honor your original commitment?
You could take credit for a team success that was mostly someone else's work. How likely are you to do this?
You disagree with a decision your team already made and announced publicly. How likely are you to support it professionally instead of undermining it behind their backs?
You promised to keep a secret, but revealing it would make you look good in front of others. How likely are you to keep your word anyway?
You realize a product or service you're selling has a flaw the buyer doesn't know about. How likely are you to disclose it before completing the sale?
You're tired and tempted to skip a safety check or step you know matters. How likely are you to do it properly anyway?
You receive money or a refund you weren't entitled to because of someone else's error. How likely are you to point it out and return it?
You're leading a project that's behind schedule, and you could quietly extend the deadline without telling stakeholders. How likely are you to communicate the delay transparently instead?
A vendor overpays you by mistake on an invoice. How likely are you to correct it rather than keep the extra amount?
You agreed to review a friend's work objectively, but it's worse than expected. How likely are you to still give an honest, fair review?
You're the only witness to a colleague cutting corners on a safety procedure. How likely are you to report it rather than stay silent?
You said you'd handle a task, but it turns out to be much harder than expected. How likely are you to still follow through rather than quietly abandon it?
A competitor's mistake could benefit you if you stay quiet about it. How likely are you to point it out anyway if it's the right thing to do?
You're managing a budget and could pad an expense report slightly. How likely are you to report exact costs instead?
You promised confidentiality about a decision, but sharing it early would make you look well-informed. How likely are you to keep the information to yourself?
A shortcut in your work would save time but slightly compromise quality. How likely are you to do it the right way instead?
You could blame a system glitch for a mistake that was actually your own. How likely are you to do this?
You're asked to enforce a policy you personally disagree with. How likely are you to apply it consistently rather than selectively ignore it?
A client offers you a personal gift in exchange for preferential treatment. How likely are you to decline and treat them the same as other clients?
You discover a flaw in your own past work that no one else has noticed. How likely are you to disclose and fix it?
You're asked to give a statement about something you witnessed. How likely are you to stick to the facts even if it's inconvenient?
A project fails partly due to your own oversight. How likely are you to acknowledge your part rather than let others take the blame?
You could sign off on work you haven't actually reviewed carefully, just to save time. How likely are you to do this?
You're offered an opportunity that came from someone else's mistake in the process. How likely are you to point out the error rather than quietly benefit?
A friend asks you to keep something private that starts to feel ethically questionable. How likely are you to voice your concern rather than just comply?
You committed to a workout partner or accountability arrangement. How likely are you to show up consistently, even when unmotivated?
You're grading your own work in a self-assessment. How likely are you to rate yourself accurately rather than inflate the score?
You could avoid consequences for a mistake by letting someone else take the fall. How likely are you to do this?
A contract has an ambiguous clause that could be interpreted in your favor unfairly. How likely are you to clarify it honestly rather than exploit the ambiguity?
You're asked to keep a commitment made by someone before you, even though you disagree with it. How likely are you to honor it rather than quietly drop it?
You realize a public claim you made turned out to be inaccurate. How likely are you to correct it, even if it's embarrassing?
You're the last line of quality control before a product ships, and you're under pressure to approve it quickly. How likely are you to insist on proper review anyway?
A friend asks you to write a reference that overstates their abilities. How likely are you to decline or write an accurate one instead?
You could exaggerate your role in a group achievement when describing it to others. How likely are you to do this?
You're managing shared funds for a group event and have full access without oversight. How likely are you to keep meticulous, transparent records anyway?
A rule exists that's inconvenient but exists for good reason. How likely are you to follow it even when no one would notice if you didn't?
You're asked to keep your word about a small favor that turned out to be more inconvenient than expected. How likely are you to still follow through?
You realize a coworker's error will be blamed on you unless you speak up. How likely are you to clarify what actually happened, fairly?
You could accept praise for something you know you didn't actually do. How likely are you to do this?
A deadline passes and you didn't finish your part, affecting the team. How likely are you to own up to it immediately rather than hope it goes unnoticed?
You're trusted with petty cash or a company card. How likely are you to use it strictly for its intended purpose?
A friend asks you to falsely confirm their alibi for something minor. How likely are you to refuse?
You're reviewing your own expenses for reimbursement and could round a few receipts up. How likely are you to submit the exact amounts?
You made a public commitment to a goal, and progress has stalled. How likely are you to be transparent about the setback rather than quietly pretend it's going well?
You could take an unearned day off by claiming to be sick when you're not. How likely are you to do this?
You're asked to represent your team's work accurately to leadership, including its shortcomings. How likely are you to give a truthful account rather than only highlight successes?
A shared responsibility falls through because you forgot your part. How likely are you to acknowledge it directly rather than let ambiguity protect you?
Someone holds a strong opinion that's very different from yours. How likely are you to listen respectfully rather than dismiss them?
A person speaks with an accent or communicates differently than you're used to. How likely are you to remain patient and respectful?
You're in a discussion with someone much younger or less experienced than you. How likely are you to take their input seriously?
Someone makes a mistake in front of a group. How likely are you to avoid embarrassing them further and instead help them discreetly?
A person's religious or cultural practice is unfamiliar to you. How likely are you to show respect for it even without fully understanding it?
Someone disagrees with you loudly during an argument. How likely are you to stay calm and avoid personal insults?
You're annoyed with a service worker who made an error. How likely are you to remain polite rather than rude?
A friend shares something personal and vulnerable with you. How likely are you to keep it private rather than share it with others?
Someone's personal space or boundaries are being tested by circumstances. How likely are you to notice and respect their comfort level?
You overhear people gossiping negatively about someone you know. How likely are you to avoid joining in?
A friend has a strong accent that's sometimes hard to understand. How likely are you to ask them to repeat rather than pretend you understood?
You're seated next to someone with very different political views at a dinner. How likely are you to engage respectfully rather than dismiss them?
A coworker has a work style very different from yours. How likely are you to respect their approach even if you'd do it differently?
Someone uses a title or pronoun for themselves that's unfamiliar to you. How likely are you to make an effort to use it correctly?
You disagree strongly with a decision made by an elder or authority figure. How likely are you to voice disagreement respectfully rather than dismissively?
A friend's home has different customs than yours, like removing shoes or eating differently. How likely are you to follow their customs as a guest?
Someone interrupts you repeatedly in a conversation. How likely are you to address it calmly rather than react with anger?
You're working with someone who has a disability and aren't sure how to be helpful. How likely are you to ask them directly what they need rather than assume?
A family member holds a belief you find outdated. How likely are you to engage with patience rather than mock or lecture them?
You're in a meeting where someone's idea is dismissed too quickly. How likely are you to ask that it be given a fair hearing?
You could mock or imitate someone's accent or mannerisms for a laugh. How likely are you to do this?
A colleague from a different generation uses technology differently than you. How likely are you to be patient rather than condescending?
Someone shares a personal struggle that makes you uncomfortable. How likely are you to listen respectfully rather than change the subject abruptly?
A stranger asks you a question that seems obvious to you. How likely are you to answer patiently rather than act annoyed?
You're working with a translator or interpreter for a conversation. How likely are you to remain patient with the extra time it takes?
You could talk over someone in a meeting because you think your idea is more important. How likely are you to do this?
A friend's cultural celebration is unfamiliar to you. How likely are you to attend with curiosity and respect rather than skepticism?
Someone gives you feedback that stings a bit. How likely are you to consider it thoughtfully rather than get defensive?
You're paired with someone for a task who has a very different working pace. How likely are you to adjust patiently rather than complain?
A person you're speaking with has a strong accent in a language that isn't their first. How likely are you to focus on what they're saying rather than how they're saying it?
You could roll your eyes or show visible impatience when someone takes longer to explain something. How likely are you to do this?
A guest at your home has different dietary restrictions. How likely are you to accommodate them without making it awkward?
Someone challenges an assumption you didn't realize you had. How likely are you to reflect on it rather than dismiss the challenge?
A coworker asks you to use a nickname or preferred name instead of their given name. How likely are you to make the effort to remember?
You're in a support role, like a teacher's aide, working with someone who needs extra patience. How likely are you to give it without frustration?
A person struggles with a task that's easy for you, and others are watching. How likely are you to help without making them feel embarrassed?
You're introduced to someone whose name is unfamiliar and hard to pronounce. How likely are you to ask politely and make an effort rather than avoid saying it?
You could exclude someone from a group conversation because their communication style is different. How likely are you to do this?
A friend's opinion on a sensitive topic differs sharply from yours. How likely are you to keep the friendship respectful despite the disagreement?
Someone corrects you in front of others. How likely are you to accept it gracefully rather than get defensive?
You're working with a team member whose first language isn't the one you're communicating in. How likely are you to simplify your language patiently rather than show frustration?
A relative's traditions around a holiday differ from what you're used to. How likely are you to participate respectfully as a guest?
Someone asks you to stop a habit that unintentionally bothers them, like a nickname or joke. How likely are you to respect their request?
You're seated near someone praying or observing a religious practice in public. How likely are you to be considerate of their space?
A younger coworker challenges your idea in a meeting. How likely are you to consider their point seriously rather than dismiss it due to their age?
You could make a sarcastic comment about someone's appearance or choices behind their back. How likely are you to do this?
You're asked to slow down your speech for someone who's hard of hearing or a non-native speaker. How likely are you to do so willingly?
A guest at an event unintentionally breaks a minor social norm. How likely are you to let it go gracefully rather than embarrass them?
Someone with a different communication style, very direct or very indirect, frustrates you slightly. How likely are you to adapt rather than judge them?
A person you respect disagrees with you on something important. How likely are you to maintain respect for them even while disagreeing?
You could win an argument by revealing something embarrassing about the other person. How likely are you to hold back to avoid hurting them?
You have information that could hurt someone's reputation if shared, even if it's true. How likely are you to think carefully before sharing it?
You're driving and could save time by speeding through a residential or school area. How likely are you to avoid doing so to keep others safe?
A joke at someone else's expense would get laughs from the group. How likely are you to avoid making it, knowing it could hurt that person?
You could exploit a loophole that harms customers but benefits your business. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You notice a product you use might be causing harm to others. How likely are you to report or warn people rather than stay silent?
In a heated argument with a loved one, you know a specific comment would deeply hurt them. How likely are you to avoid saying it even if you're angry?
You have the chance to prank someone in a way that could genuinely scare or humiliate them. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You could spread a rumor that isn't fully confirmed but would be entertaining to share. How likely are you to avoid spreading it to prevent possible harm?
You're frustrated with a pet or child's behavior. How likely are you to respond calmly rather than harshly?
You know a piece of gossip that would hurt a friend if it got back to them. How likely are you to make sure it doesn't spread further?
You're annoyed with a customer service worker who isn't at fault. How likely are you to keep your frustration in check rather than take it out on them?
A competitor in a game or sport is clearly overmatched. How likely are you to ease up rather than humiliate them?
You have leverage in a negotiation that could be used to pressure the other side unfairly. How likely are you to negotiate in good faith instead?
You could post an embarrassing photo of a friend online as a joke. How likely are you to ask permission first or avoid it?
A heated online debate gives you the chance to say something cutting. How likely are you to keep the conversation civil instead?
You're driving and someone cuts you off. How likely are you to let it go rather than retaliate aggressively?
You know a secret that could end someone's relationship if revealed carelessly. How likely are you to handle it with extreme care?
A child in your care is misbehaving in public. How likely are you to discipline them calmly rather than out of anger or embarrassment?
You could 'accidentally' let slip something hurtful about someone during an argument. How likely are you to hold back?
You could publicly call out someone's mistake in a humiliating way instead of privately. How likely are you to do this?
You have the ability to sabotage a rival's opportunity without getting caught. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
A pet is misbehaving and testing your patience. How likely are you to use gentle correction rather than harsh punishment?
You're upset with a family member and know exactly which insecurity to target to hurt them. How likely are you to avoid going there even in anger?
A group chat is planning to prank someone in a way that could genuinely embarrass them. How likely are you to speak up against it?
You could report a minor rule violation that would get someone in trouble disproportionate to the offense. How likely are you to handle it more proportionately instead?
You could send an angry message in the heat of the moment instead of cooling down first. How likely are you to send it right away?
You're aware that a harmless-seeming prank could genuinely scare someone with anxiety. How likely are you to reconsider it?
A coworker's mistake is minor, but publicly pointing it out would make you look good. How likely are you to address it privately instead?
You have information that could end someone's career if leaked, even though it's only tangentially related to their competence. How likely are you to keep it confidential?
You're in an argument that's escalating, and you know a below-the-belt comment would 'win' it. How likely are you to avoid using it?
A neighbor's noise is bothering you. How likely are you to address it calmly rather than retaliate with your own disruptive behavior?
You could deliberately leave someone out of an important group or event to hurt their feelings. How likely are you to do this?
You witness a heated argument between strangers escalating. How likely are you to try to de-escalate rather than encourage it for entertainment?
A friend asks for brutally honest feedback on something deeply personal, like their appearance. How likely are you to soften your delivery to avoid unnecessary hurt?
You could physically startle or scare someone as a joke, knowing they might overreact. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You're moderating a discussion where things are getting personal. How likely are you to step in and redirect toward respectful disagreement?
You could threaten to end a friendship or relationship just to win an argument. How likely are you to do this?
A sibling or close friend is an easy target for teasing about a sensitive topic. How likely are you to avoid it even when others are laughing?
You have the chance to expose someone's private struggle publicly to make a point. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You're annoyed and tempted to slam a door or throw something to vent frustration around others. How likely are you to find a calmer outlet instead?
A prank war with a friend is escalating toward something that could actually damage property or hurt someone. How likely are you to call it off?
You could 'accidentally' forward an embarrassing message to the wrong group to humiliate someone. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You're in a position to give someone a harsh performance review that goes beyond what's fair, out of personal frustration. How likely are you to keep it strictly professional and accurate?
A stray comment from you could seriously damage someone's confidence right before a big moment. How likely are you to hold back?
You could use physical intimidation, even jokingly, to make a point in an argument. How likely are you to do this?
You're frustrated with customer service on the phone. How likely are you to stay civil rather than raise your voice at the person helping you?
A friend's new haircut or outfit is a genuinely easy target for mockery in front of others. How likely are you to resist making the joke?
You have the option to 'accidentally' exclude someone's name from thank-you credits out of spite. How likely are you to make sure they're properly credited instead?
You're tempted to bring up an old mistake of someone's during an unrelated argument just to hurt them. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You witness littering or environmental damage happening in public. How likely are you to say something or pick up the litter yourself?
There's a local community cleanup or volunteer event. How likely are you to participate even though it's not required of you?
You see a safety hazard in a public space, like a spill or broken step. How likely are you to alert someone or take steps to prevent injury?
Voting or civic participation requires effort but seems minor as an individual act. How likely are you to still participate?
A neighbor needs help you're capable of giving, such as during a storm or emergency. How likely are you to offer assistance?
You could ignore a broken public facility that isn't technically your responsibility. How likely are you to report it anyway?
You witness a scam or fraud attempt targeting a vulnerable person nearby. How likely are you to intervene or alert authorities?
A local charity asks for a small donation of time or money. How likely are you to contribute something, even if small?
You see potential danger to public safety, like unsecured equipment at a playground. How likely are you to report it rather than assume someone else will?
You could conserve shared resources like electricity or water even when no one is checking. How likely are you to do so anyway?
A local election has low turnout expected, and your vote feels insignificant. How likely are you to vote anyway?
You notice a broken streetlight or pothole in your neighborhood. How likely are you to report it to the local authority?
A community survey asks for your honest input on a local issue. How likely are you to take the time to respond thoughtfully?
You're asked to serve on a volunteer board or committee that takes up your free time. How likely are you to say yes if you're able to?
A public restroom or shared space is left messy by someone else. How likely are you to leave it better than you found it, within reason?
You notice a local business struggling and could support it with your patronage. How likely are you to choose it over a larger chain?
A neighborhood watch group needs volunteers for occasional patrols. How likely are you to participate?
You witness unsafe construction or work practices in a public area. How likely are you to report it to the relevant authority?
A blood donation drive is happening near you, and you're eligible to donate. How likely are you to participate?
You notice a public park or shared space becoming neglected. How likely are you to bring it up at a community meeting?
You could avoid jury duty or civic obligations by making excuses, even when you're able to serve. How likely are you to do this?
A local school or shelter is requesting donated supplies. How likely are you to contribute something you can spare?
You see a public safety campaign, like a fire safety drive, and could help spread the word. How likely are you to share it with others?
A community garden or shared space needs occasional upkeep. How likely are you to pitch in even without being asked?
You're asked to mentor someone younger or less experienced in your field. How likely are you to make time for it?
A local disaster relief effort needs volunteers. How likely are you to offer your time or skills?
You notice a public utility, like a water fountain, is broken. How likely are you to report it rather than assume it's already known?
A community petition addresses an issue you genuinely care about. How likely are you to sign it after reading it carefully?
You could park illegally in a way that inconveniences others because it's more convenient for you. How likely are you to do this?
You're invited to a town hall meeting about a local issue that affects your neighborhood. How likely are you to attend and share your view?
A local library or public institution needs volunteers for a literacy program. How likely are you to consider volunteering?
You notice a public sign or notice with outdated or incorrect information. How likely are you to flag it to the responsible party?
A community fundraiser for a public cause, like a playground, needs contributions. How likely are you to contribute what you can?
You're asked to be a witness for a neighbor's minor civic matter, like signing a document. How likely are you to help if you can?
A recycling or composting program is available but requires a bit more effort than regular trash. How likely are you to participate?
You could ignore a mandatory jury summons and hope it goes unnoticed. How likely are you to do this?
You're aware of a public health guideline, like staying home when contagious, that's inconvenient to follow. How likely are you to follow it anyway for others' sake?
A local road safety issue, like a dangerous crosswalk, could be fixed if enough residents raise it. How likely are you to add your voice?
You're asked to help organize a neighborhood event, like a block party. How likely are you to volunteer some of your time?
A public awareness campaign for a cause you care about needs people to share accurate information. How likely are you to help spread it correctly?
You notice an elderly or disabled neighbor who might need help with errands. How likely are you to check in and offer occasional assistance?
A local animal shelter needs fosters or volunteers. How likely are you to consider helping if you're able?
You're asked to complete a census or official survey that helps allocate community resources. How likely are you to fill it out accurately?
A public meeting addresses a decision that mostly affects a group other than your own. How likely are you to still show up in support if you can?
You notice safety equipment, like a fire extinguisher in a shared building, is missing or expired. How likely are you to report it?
A local mentorship or tutoring program for underprivileged students needs volunteers. How likely are you to sign up if you have time?
You're asked to help a new immigrant family in your community get oriented. How likely are you to offer some of your time?
A community cleanup after a natural disaster needs extra hands. How likely are you to show up and help?
You notice a public bench or shared amenity vandalized. How likely are you to report it rather than ignore it?
A local initiative asks residents to reduce noise or disruption during certain hours out of consideration for others. How likely are you to comply even when inconvenient?
You have the option to walk, bike, or take public transport instead of driving for a short trip. How likely are you to choose the more eco-friendly option?
You're finished with a plastic item, and a recycling bin is slightly farther away than a trash bin. How likely are you to still walk to the recycling bin?
You notice a company you support has poor environmental practices. How likely are you to factor that into your decision to keep supporting them?
You could leave lights, water, or appliances running unnecessarily. How likely are you to turn them off to reduce waste?
You're offered a product with excessive, wasteful packaging versus a more sustainable option at a slightly higher cost. How likely are you to choose the sustainable option?
You see someone littering in a natural area like a park or beach. How likely are you to speak up or pick up the trash yourself?
You could reduce resource-intensive consumption for environmental reasons, even if it's less convenient. How likely are you to make that choice sometimes?
A gathering you're hosting could use single-use plastics for convenience or reusable items with more effort. How likely are you to choose reusable items?
You learn that a habit of yours has a meaningfully negative environmental impact. How likely are you to try to change that habit?
You have the chance to plant a tree or support a small environmental project with your time. How likely are you to get involved?
You're buying a new appliance and could choose a slightly cheaper, less energy-efficient model. How likely are you to pick the more efficient one instead?
You notice your household produces a lot of food waste. How likely are you to try composting or reducing it?
A store offers a free plastic bag, but you have a reusable one in your car. How likely are you to go get it instead of taking the plastic one?
You're choosing between a direct flight and a more eco-friendly but longer travel option. How likely are you to consider the environmental impact in your decision?
A local initiative asks residents to reduce water usage during a drought. How likely are you to comply even if it's inconvenient?
You notice a leak or running toilet wasting water in your home. How likely are you to fix it promptly rather than let it slide?
You're deciding whether to repair an item or throw it away and buy new. How likely are you to consider repairing it first for environmental reasons?
You could leave your car engine idling for a long time out of convenience, even when it's not necessary. How likely are you to do this?
A workplace initiative asks employees to print less and go digital. How likely are you to make the effort to comply?
You're shopping for produce, and local, in-season options are less convenient than imported ones. How likely are you to choose local when you can?
You notice your neighborhood doesn't have accessible recycling, so you could just throw everything in the trash. How likely are you to find another way to recycle anyway?
A company offers a discount for opting into paperless billing. How likely are you to switch, given the environmental as well as financial benefit?
You're at an event with excessive food waste being thrown out. How likely are you to suggest donating leftovers rather than discarding them?
You could use significantly more water than necessary for a long shower or car wash without a second thought. How likely are you to do this?
You're choosing a mode of transport for a daily commute. How likely are you to factor in the environmental impact of your choice?
A community initiative is planting trees to offset local emissions. How likely are you to participate or contribute?
You notice your workplace or school doesn't recycle properly. How likely are you to raise the issue with someone who can fix it?
You're buying clothes and could choose a more sustainably made option at a higher price. How likely are you to consider sustainability in your purchase?
A friend asks you to join a beach or river cleanup event. How likely are you to say yes?
You notice single-use plastic items you could easily replace with reusable alternatives. How likely are you to make that switch over time?
You could keep buying disposable items purely for convenience, even when reusable options are just as easy. How likely are you to do this?
You're aware that a product you love is made in an environmentally harmful way. How likely are you to look for a more sustainable alternative?
A local composting program is available at your workplace or neighborhood. How likely are you to participate?
You notice a wildfire or environmental risk warning that asks residents to conserve resources. How likely are you to comply promptly?
You're deciding whether to plant native, water-efficient plants or a high-maintenance lawn. How likely are you to choose the more sustainable option?
A friend suggests carpooling to reduce emissions on a regular trip you both make. How likely are you to agree to it?
You notice excessive packaging on an online order you placed. How likely are you to mention it as feedback to the company?
A local farmers market offers seasonal produce with less packaging than the grocery store. How likely are you to choose it when convenient?
You're offered the choice between a paper and digital receipt. How likely are you to choose the digital option?
You could leave electronics plugged in and drawing power unnecessarily, even when you know it wastes energy. How likely are you to do this?
A neighborhood initiative asks residents to carpool or use public transit one day a week to reduce traffic emissions. How likely are you to participate?
You're buying a gift and could choose a more sustainably packaged option, even if slightly less convenient. How likely are you to choose it?
You notice a company's environmental claims seem exaggerated or misleading. How likely are you to research further before trusting them?
A local park cleanup is happening on a weekend you'd normally spend relaxing. How likely are you to give up some of that time to participate?
You're deciding on home insulation or energy upgrades that cost more upfront but save energy long-term. How likely are you to consider the environmental benefit?
You notice a friend or family member wasting a lot of food regularly. How likely are you to gently bring it up?
A workplace event could use compostable dishware instead of plastic. How likely are you to suggest the more sustainable option if you're involved in planning?
You're choosing between products with excessive versus minimal packaging for the same price. How likely are you to pick the one with less packaging?
A community solar or renewable energy program is available to you at a modest cost. How likely are you to consider signing up?
You notice your daily habits produce more waste than necessary. How likely are you to make a conscious effort to reduce it over time?
You find a way to access a paid service or content for free without permission. How likely are you to do this?
You receive someone's private message or photo that wasn't meant for you. How likely are you to keep it confidential rather than share it further?
You could use someone else's account or password without their explicit permission, even for a small task. How likely are you to do this?
You see misinformation being spread online by someone you know. How likely are you to point out the correct information respectfully?
You could copy someone else's creative work, like writing, art, or code, and present it as your own. How likely are you to do this?
An anonymous online setting makes it easier to be harsh or insulting to others. How likely are you to remain respectful anyway?
You have access to a friend's device or account by accident. How likely are you to avoid looking through their private information?
You could leave an exaggerated or dishonest online review to help or hurt a business. How likely are you to do this?
A platform collects more of your friends' or contacts' data than necessary when you sign up. How likely are you to think about their privacy before agreeing?
You see cyberbullying happening in a comment section or group chat. How likely are you to intervene or report it?
You could use an AI tool to write an assignment or work report and submit it as entirely your own. How likely are you to do this?
You notice a friend's social media account might be hacked, sending odd messages. How likely are you to warn them rather than ignore it?
You could screenshot and share a private conversation without the other person's consent. How likely are you to do this?
A website asks for more personal data than seems necessary for its service. How likely are you to think twice before providing it?
You could use a fake name or persona online to say things you wouldn't say under your real identity. How likely are you to do this?
You notice a friend oversharing sensitive information online that could put them at risk. How likely are you to gently suggest caution?
You could download copyrighted movies or music without paying, even though free legal alternatives exist. How likely are you to do this?
A group chat is sharing someone's private photo without their consent. How likely are you to object or refuse to forward it?
You could use a friend's streaming account without their knowledge to save money. How likely are you to do this?
You're reviewing a product online and had a mediocre but not terrible experience. How likely are you to write an honest, balanced review rather than an extreme one?
You could pretend to be someone else online to prank or deceive a stranger. How likely are you to do this?
A friend asks you to help them create a fake account to spy on someone. How likely are you to refuse?
You notice AI-generated content being passed off as real without disclosure. How likely are you to point it out if it's misleading?
You could use facial recognition or tracking tools to find information about someone without their knowledge, just out of curiosity. How likely are you to do this?
A friend's old social media post resurfaces and could be used to embarrass them. How likely are you to avoid sharing it further?
You're asked to review terms of service before agreeing, but skipping it is faster. How likely are you to actually check for concerning data practices?
You could click on a suspicious link forwarded by a friend without warning them it might be a scam. How likely are you to warn them instead?
You notice a website using dark patterns to trick users into subscriptions. How likely are you to point this out to others if asked?
You could use someone's personal photos from social media without credit for your own project. How likely are you to do this?
A friend asks for your opinion on whether to post something that could embarrass a third person. How likely are you to advise against it?
You could keep a group chat's embarrassing content saved to use against someone later. How likely are you to do this?
You're aware that an online petition or campaign you're about to share contains false information. How likely are you to verify before sharing?
A friend wants to use AI to generate a fake image of someone for a joke. How likely are you to discourage it if it could be misleading or hurtful?
You could bypass a website's age verification or terms by lying about your details. How likely are you to do this?
You notice your data being used in ways you didn't expect after signing up for a service. How likely are you to look into your privacy settings and adjust them?
A friend asks you to help them create a fake positive review for their business. How likely are you to decline?
You could publicly post a heated argument you had privately with someone to get sympathy online. How likely are you to keep it private instead?
You're moderating an online community and a friend breaks a rule. How likely are you to apply the same consequence you would to a stranger?
You could use a company's free trial repeatedly with fake information to avoid ever paying. How likely are you to do this?
A friend shares a conspiracy theory online that you know is false. How likely are you to gently share accurate information?
You notice a scam targeting elderly or vulnerable people in your online community. How likely are you to warn others about it?
You could use someone's likeness or voice with AI tools without their permission for entertainment. How likely are you to avoid doing this?
You're asked to beta test software and find a security flaw. How likely are you to report it responsibly rather than exploit or ignore it?
A friend's location is visible to you through an app, and they don't know you can see it. How likely are you to mention it rather than use the information secretly?
You could leave a one-star review for a business based on a single bad experience without giving context. How likely are you to write a fair, detailed review instead?
You notice an online store using misleading countdown timers to pressure purchases. How likely are you to point this out if someone asks your opinion?
A friend asks you to like and comment on all their posts to boost engagement, even ones you don't actually like. How likely are you to only engage genuinely?
You could quietly read a family member's private messages if you had access. How likely are you to resist the temptation?
You're aware that an app you use collects more data than users realize. How likely are you to inform friends who ask about it?
A friend wants to publicly shame someone online for a mistake instead of addressing it privately. How likely are you to suggest a more private approach?
A close friend confides a mistake they made. How likely are you to support them without judgment while still being honest with them?
Someone you trust asks you to do something that conflicts with your personal values. How likely are you to say no despite the relationship?
A friend is being talked about negatively while they're not present. How likely are you to defend them or stay neutral rather than join in?
You're asked to keep a promise to a friend that has become inconvenient over time. How likely are you to keep it anyway?
Someone betrayed your trust in the past, but they're now sincerely trying to make amends. How likely are you to give them a fair chance?
Your loyalty to a friend or team is tested when they've clearly done something wrong. How likely are you to hold them accountable rather than cover for them?
A colleague or friend asks for your support in a difficult time, even if it costs you personally. How likely are you to show up for them?
You discover a friend has been dishonest with you, but they don't know you're aware. How likely are you to address it directly rather than letting resentment build?
You're given confidential information by someone who trusts you. How likely are you to protect that trust even under pressure to reveal it?
A group you belong to pressures you to go along with something you believe is wrong. How likely are you to stay true to your values rather than conform?
A friend confides in you about a mistake and asks you not to tell anyone, including their partner. How likely are you to respect that unless it involves real danger?
You're asked to choose sides in a disagreement between two close friends. How likely are you to stay fair and avoid taking sides unnecessarily?
A business partner makes a decision you disagree with but that was made in good faith. How likely are you to support them publicly while raising concerns privately?
You're trusted with a spare key to a friend's home. How likely are you to use it strictly for its intended purpose?
A friend shares a password with you for a one-time task. How likely are you to avoid using it beyond that purpose?
You're asked to keep a surprise party secret from the guest of honor. How likely are you to keep the secret until the reveal?
A team member is struggling, and covering for them repeatedly could hurt the whole team. How likely are you to address it directly rather than continue quietly covering?
You're the keeper of a joint fund or shared account with a friend. How likely are you to manage it with full transparency?
A close friend asks for your unbiased opinion on a big decision, even though you might disagree with them. How likely are you to be honest rather than just supportive?
You're asked to safeguard sensitive family information during a difficult time, like an illness. How likely are you to protect it as requested?
You could share a friend's confided secret with someone else because it's 'juicy' gossip. How likely are you to do this?
A friend who's usually reliable lets you down unexpectedly. How likely are you to give them the benefit of the doubt and ask what happened?
You're part of a team that made a collective mistake, and it would be easy to let one person take the blame. How likely are you to make sure responsibility is shared fairly?
A friend asks you to help them keep a personal goal accountable. How likely are you to follow through on checking in with them?
You're trusted with reviewing a friend's personal writing, like a journal excerpt, for feedback. How likely are you to handle it with care and confidentiality?
A long-time friend makes a life choice you disagree with. How likely are you to remain supportive of them as a person, even while disagreeing?
You could quietly distance yourself from a friend the moment they're going through a difficult, less convenient time. How likely are you to do this?
You're asked to be someone's emergency contact. How likely are you to take that responsibility seriously and stay reachable?
A friend trusted you with a difficult confession about their past. How likely are you to treat them the same way you did before, without judgment?
You're aware that a mutual friend is spreading a false rumor about someone you both know. How likely are you to correct it rather than stay silent?
A colleague asks you to keep their job search confidential while they're still employed elsewhere. How likely are you to honor that request?
You're part of a group project where one member's mistake could be pinned on the group. How likely are you to advocate for accurate accountability rather than a scapegoat?
You could break a promise to a friend the moment a more appealing option comes along. How likely are you to do this?
A friend going through a hard time needs consistency from you, even when it's inconvenient. How likely are you to keep showing up for them?
You're trusted by a family member to make a decision on their behalf during an emergency. How likely are you to act in their best interest rather than your own convenience?
A close friend's ex reaches out to you for information about them. How likely are you to respect your friend's privacy and decline to share?
You're asked to store something important, like a document or valuable item, for a friend temporarily. How likely are you to keep it safe and return it exactly as given?
A friend confided that they're struggling financially and asked you to keep it private. How likely are you to respect that even if others ask about them?
You're part of a close group where one member is being unfairly excluded by others. How likely are you to advocate for including them?
You could use private information a friend shared with you to gain an advantage over them later. How likely are you to do this?
A friend asks you to be honest about whether they can trust a third person. How likely are you to give a thoughtful, honest answer rather than avoid the question?
You're the designated decision-maker for a group trip or event. How likely are you to consider everyone's input fairly rather than just your own preferences?
A friend who helped you in the past now needs help themselves. How likely are you to prioritize supporting them in return?
You're trusted with planning a surprise for someone, and a shortcut would risk spoiling it. How likely are you to stick to the plan carefully?
A family member asks you to keep a difficult diagnosis private until they're ready to share it themselves. How likely are you to respect their timeline?
You're aware a friend is being deceived by their partner. How likely are you to find a caring way to bring it up rather than stay silent?
A friend loans you something valuable and trusts you to take care of it. How likely are you to treat it with extra care?
You're asked to represent a group's interests in a negotiation. How likely are you to advocate for the group rather than your own personal gain?
A close friend is going through something you don't fully understand. How likely are you to stay patient and present rather than pull away?
You're the one person a friend trusts with a difficult truth about their family. How likely are you to hold that trust carefully and never use it against them?
